methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Genespire is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Luigi Naldini and Alessio Cantore, with $52M in total capital raised. Valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same stage, plus 3 public sector comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $52M total raised proxy, yielding $99M. Peer-median methodology anchors to five same-stage, same-sector private comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—producing a median of $810M. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the median sector P/S across the three public comparables is 5.8x, but Genespire's revenue is not on file. This is typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated range is $84M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $454M derived from the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in peer selection and stage-progression assumptions. Key risks to valuation include the significant heterogeneity within the Series B biotech peer set (range $600M–$3.3B) and lack of revenue visibility to calibrate public-market multiples. A tighter range would require additional data on clinical-stage progression, IP positioning, or recent market comparables.
